The kernel crashed when doing following operations.

[root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n tbf nid > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_policies;
[root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]#  cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule;
regular_requests:
high_priority_requests:
[root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n start xixi {0@lo} 1 > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule;
[root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]#  cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule;

Patch Set 2:

Li Xi reported a crash. It is in the above comments.

The kernel crashed when doing following operations.Not the easiest to read but below is his commands. I haven't got around to testing and fixing it.

[root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n tbf nid > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_policies; [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; regular_requests: high_priority_requests: [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n start xixi {0@lo} 1 > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; regular_requests: high_priority_requests: [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; regular_requests: high_priority_requests: [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n start xixi1 {0@lo} 1 > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# echo -n start xixi2 {0@lo} 1 > /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule; [root@server1 2.5.56-tbf-proc-change-9384]# cat /proc/fs/lustre/ost/OSS/ost_io/nrs_tbf_rule;

Patch Set 2:

Li I can now duplicate the crashes you see. Looking into a fix.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Yes, I know why it crashed. It is really a mess.

Patch Set 2:

Actually Yang you have nicer words for this work than I do :-) Next patch is coming which will not crash. The problem is without proper docs I can only do limited testing and I don't know what the expected output/input format is. So Li please try it and tell me what is wrong.

The good news Li is that their is no size limitation to seq_file which was not the case for the old proc support. The bug you see if from some thing else.

parents.subject: LU-3319 procfs: move osp proc handling to seq_files

commit.subject: LU-3319 procfs: Move NRS TBF proc handling to seq_files
commit.message:
LU-3319 procfs: Move NRS TBF proc handling to seq_files

With newer kernels moving their proc file system handling
to seq_files this patch migrates the proc handling for NRS
TBF to this new approach.

Change-Id: I121755b611296bf7b9527de65d0e6cf8c4980151
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9384
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
